test_cases.description: NewPATHandler returns a non-nil *PATHandler when both database and registry parameters are non-nil.
preconditions: A valid *db.DB instance is available (in-memory SQLite)
A valid *auth.PermissionRegistry instance is initialized with built-in permissions
test_cases.expected: NewPATHandler returns a non-nil *PATHandler; no panic occurs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: handler = NewPATHandler(db, registry); assert handler != nil

test_cases.description: RegisterRoutes registers all four PAT lifecycle routes on the provided Echo group.
preconditions: A valid *PATHandler has been constructed
An Echo instance is initialized with an authenticated API group via server.APIGroup()
test_cases.expected: Four routes are registered: POST /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens/:token_id, DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: handler.RegisterRoutes(group); routes = echo.Routes(); assert routeExists(routes, 'POST', '/user/tokens'); assert routeExists(routes, 'GET', '/user/tokens'); assert routeExists(routes, 'GET', '/user/tokens/:token_id'); assert routeExists(routes, 'DELETE', '/user/tokens/:token_id')

test_cases.description: All four PAT endpoints return HTTP 401 for unauthenticated requests, handled by the auth middleware.
preconditions: Echo server is running with auth middleware installed on the API group
No Authorization header is provided in the request
test_cases.expected: All four endpoints return HTTP 401 before any handler code executes
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each endpoint in [POST /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens/abc12345, DELETE /user/tokens/abc12345]: resp = sendRequest(method, path, noAuthHeader); assert resp.statusCode == 401

test_cases.description: All four PAT endpoints set Cache-Control: no-store on every response including error responses.
preconditions: Echo server is running with PAT routes registered
Auth middleware is installed and a valid credential is available
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: Each response contains the header Cache-Control: no-store regardless of the HTTP status code returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each (method, path, body) in testCases: resp = sendRequest(method, path, body, validAPIKey); assert resp.headers['Cache-Control'] == 'no-store'

test_cases.description: generateTokenID returns exactly an 8-character string composed only of characters from [a-z0-9].
preconditions: crypto/rand is available (standard Go environment)
test_cases.expected: Returns a string of length 8 where every character is in the set [a-z0-9]; no error is returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: id, err = generateTokenID(); assert err == nil; assert len(id) == 8; for each char in id: assert char in 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789'

test_cases.description: generateSecret returns exactly a 32-character string composed only of characters from [a-z0-9].
preconditions: crypto/rand is available (standard Go environment)
test_cases.expected: Returns a string of length 32 where every character is in the set [a-z0-9]; no error is returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: secret, err = generateSecret(); assert err == nil; assert len(secret) == 32; for each char in secret: assert char in 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789'

test_cases.description: hashSecret is deterministic and returns a lowercase 64-character hex string for any given input.
test_cases.expected: Returns the lowercase hex string '2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824'; calling twice with the same input returns the same result
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: hash1 = hashSecret('hello'); hash2 = hashSecret('hello'); assert hash1 == hash2; assert hash1 == '2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824'; assert len(hash1) == 64; assert hash1 == toLower(hash1)

test_cases.description: The full PAT token is constructed in the format <TokenPrefix>_pat_<token_id>_<secret> matching the regex ^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$.
preconditions: apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak' (default)
test_cases.expected: Token string equals 'ak_pat_a1b2c3d4_deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef' and matches regex ^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: token = buildToken(apikit.TokenPrefix, tokenID, secret); assert token == 'ak_pat_a1b2c3d4_deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef'; assert matchesRegex(token, '^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$')

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'name is required' when the name field is missing or empty.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
PermissionRegistry is initialized with standard permissions
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"name is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'name is required'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'name must be 255 characters or fewer' when name exceeds 255 characters.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"name must be 255 characters or fewer"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: longName = 'a' * 256; resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:longName, permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'name must be 255 characters or fewer'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'permissions are required' when permissions array is missing or empty.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 for both missing and empty permissions array; message is 'permissions are required'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 400; assert resp1.json().error.message == 'permissions are required'; resp2 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:[], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 400; assert resp2.json().error.message == 'permissions are required'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'invalid permission format: <permission>' for a permission string with no colon.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
PermissionRegistry is initialized
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with message 'invalid permission format: usersread'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['usersread'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'invalid permission format: usersread'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'unknown permission: <permission>' for a well-formed but unregistered permission.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
PermissionRegistry is initialized with standard permissions only (does not include 'widgets:delete')
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with message 'unknown permission: widgets:delete'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['widgets:delete'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'unknown permission: widgets:delete'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90' for an invalid expires value.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with message 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for invalidExpires in [7, 365, 999, 1, -1]: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:invalidExpires}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 with message 'invalid request body' when the JSON body cannot be decoded.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with message 'invalid request body'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', rawBody='not valid json {', contentType='application/json', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'invalid request body'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT treats a missing expires field as a default of 90 days and sets expires_at to created_at + 90*24h.
preconditions: Valid authenticated user context is injected (API key credential)
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 response where expires_at equals created_at + 2160 hours (90 days)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['tokens:read']}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; createdAt = parseTime(resp.json().created_at); expiresAt = parseTime(resp.json().expires_at); assert expiresAt == createdAt + 90*24*hours

test_cases.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 403 with message 'cannot grant permission: <permission>' when a PAT credential requests a permission not in its own permissions list.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage', 'users:read']
PermissionRegistry includes 'keys:manage'
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with message 'cannot grant permission: keys:manage'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['keys:manage'], expires:30}, auth=patWithTokensManageAndUsersRead); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.json().error.message == 'cannot grant permission: keys:manage'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT skips privilege escalation check entirely when the authenticated credential is an API key.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is an API key (admin or regular)
PermissionRegistry includes all standard permissions
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; no privilege escalation error returned; PAT is created successfully
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['users:read','keys:manage','tokens:manage'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.json().token_id != ''

test_cases.description: CreatePAT validates permissions against the registry before checking privilege escalation.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage']
PermissionRegistry does NOT include 'widgets:delete'
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with 'unknown permission: widgets:delete' (registry check fires before escalation check, so 400 not 403)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['widgets:delete'], expires:30}, auth=patCredential); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'unknown permission: widgets:delete'

test_cases.description: CreatePAT generates token_id and secret, hashes secret, constructs token, inserts record, and returns HTTP 201 with CreatePATResponse.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user with user_id 'user-1' via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'users:read' and 'orgs:read'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with CreatePATResponse containing token_id (8 chars), name 'ci-deploy', token matching ^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$, permissions ['users:read','orgs:read'], non-null expires_at, and created_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['users:read','orgs:read'], expires:90}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; body = resp.json(); assert len(body.token_id) == 8; assert body.name == 'ci-deploy'; assert matchesRegex(body.token, '^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$'); assert body.permissions == ['users:read','orgs:read']; assert body.expires_at != null; assert body.created_at != ''

test_cases.description: CreatePAT stores only the SHA-256 hash of the secret in the pats table, never the plaintext.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
test_cases.expected: The pats table row has secret_hash = SHA-256(secret) and does not contain the plaintext secret string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); body = resp.json(); parts = split(body.token, '_pat_'); secret = last part of token after last '_'; row = db.queryOne('SELECT secret_hash FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', body.token_id); expectedHash = sha256Hex(secret); assert row.secret_hash == expectedHash; assert row.secret_hash != secret

test_cases.description: CreatePAT preserves the permissions array in insertion order in both the response and the database.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'orgs:read', 'users:read', 'tokens:read'
test_cases.expected: Response permissions array equals ['orgs:read', 'tokens:read', 'users:read'] in that exact order; pats table stores JSON array in same order
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['orgs:read','tokens:read','users:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.json().permissions == ['orgs:read','tokens:read','users:read']; row = db.queryOne('SELECT permissions FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', resp.json().token_id); assert jsonParse(row.permissions) == ['orgs:read','tokens:read','users:read']

test_cases.description: CreatePATResponse omits the revoked_at and expires_days fields from the HTTP 201 response.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 response JSON does not contain keys 'revoked_at' or 'expires_days'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; respKeys = keys(resp.json()); assert 'revoked_at' not in respKeys; assert 'expires_days' not in respKeys

test_cases.description: CreatePAT wraps the pats table INSERT in a db.WithTx transaction so failures roll back automatically.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
A mock or stub db.WithTx is configured to simulate a transaction failure mid-INSERT
Authenticated user via API key

input.db_behavior: WithTx callback returns an error simulating DB failure

test_cases.expected: HTTP 500 is returned; pats table contains no new row; transaction was rolled back
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: injectDBFailure(); resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'; rowCount = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats'); assert rowCount == 0

test_cases.description: CreatePAT requires tokens:manage permission; a PAT credential lacking this permission receives HTTP 403.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:read'] only (no 'tokens:manage')
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; no PAT is created in the database
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=patWithOnlyTokensRead); assert resp.statusCode == 403; rowCount = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats'); assert rowCount == 0

test_cases.description: CreatePAT allows duplicate PAT names for the same user; both PATs are persisted successfully.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
test_cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 201 with distinct token_ids; two rows exist in the pats table both with name 'ci-deploy'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 201; resp2 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'ci-deploy', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:60}, auth=apiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 201; assert resp1.json().token_id != resp2.json().token_id; rows = db.query('SELECT * FROM pats WHERE name = ?', 'ci-deploy'); assert len(rows) == 2

test_cases.description: ListPATs returns HTTP 200 with all PATs for the authenticated user ordered by created_at DESC.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User 'user-1' has three PATs created at different timestamps
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array of 3 PATResponse objects ordered newest-first by created_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; pats = resp.json(); assert len(pats) == 3; assert pats[0].created_at >= pats[1].created_at >= pats[2].created_at

test_cases.description: ListPATs includes expired and revoked PATs in the response without filtering them out.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User has one active PAT, one expired PAT (expires_at in the past), and one revoked PAT (revoked_at set)
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with array of 3 PATs; one has non-null revoked_at, one has expired expires_at, one is active
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; pats = resp.json(); assert len(pats) == 3; revokedPATs = filter(pats, p => p.revoked_at != null); expiredPATs = filter(pats, p => p.expires_at != null && parseTime(p.expires_at) < now()); assert len(revokedPATs) == 1; assert len(expiredPATs) >= 1

test_cases.description: ListPATs response does not include secret_hash, plaintext token, or expires_days fields in any array element.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User has at least one PAT in the pats table
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: Response JSON array elements have no keys named 'secret_hash', 'token', or 'expires_days'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; for each pat in resp.json(): assert 'secret_hash' not in keys(pat); assert 'token' not in keys(pat); assert 'expires_days' not in keys(pat)

test_cases.description: ListPATs returns only PATs belonging to the authenticated user; other users' PATs are excluded.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User 'user-1' has 2 PATs; User 'user-2' has 3 PATs
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with only 2 PATs belonging to user-1; user-2's PATs are not present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; pats = resp.json(); assert len(pats) == 2; for each pat in pats: assert db.queryOne('SELECT user_id FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', pat.token_id).user_id == 'user-1'

test_cases.description: ListPATs requires tokens:read permission; PAT credential lacking it receives HTTP 403.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage'] only (no 'tokens:read')
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=patWithOnlyTokensManage); assert resp.statusCode == 403

test_cases.description: GetPAT returns HTTP 200 with PATResponse metadata for a valid token_id belonging to the authenticated user.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User 'user-1' has a PAT with token_id 'abc12345'
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with PATResponse containing token_id 'abc12345', correct name, permissions, created_at, expires_at, and revoked_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; body = resp.json(); assert body.token_id == 'abc12345'; assert 'token' not in keys(body); assert 'secret_hash' not in keys(body); assert body.created_at != ''

test_cases.description: GetPAT returns HTTP 404 with message 'token not found' when the token_id does not exist for the authenticated user.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table (empty or without 'nonexistent' token)
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"token not found"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens/nonexistent', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.json().error.message == 'token not found'

test_cases.description: GetPAT requires tokens:read permission; PAT credential lacking it receives HTTP 403.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage'] only
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=patWithOnlyTokensManage); assert resp.statusCode == 403

test_cases.description: RevokePAT issues a conditional UPDATE, sets revoked_at, and returns HTTP 200 with updated PATResponse.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User 'user-1' has an active (non-revoked) PAT with token_id 'abc12345'
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:manage
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with PATResponse where revoked_at is a non-null timestamp; pats table row for abc12345 has revoked_at set
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; body = resp.json(); assert body.revoked_at != null; row = db.queryOne('SELECT revoked_at FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', 'abc12345'); assert row.revoked_at != null

test_cases.description: RevokePAT returns HTTP 404 when no row matches token_id AND user_id, and HTTP 400 when row exists but is already revoked.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
A revoked PAT with token_id 'revoked01' exists for user-1
No PAT with token_id 'missing00' exists
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:manage
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 for missing token; HTTP 400 with 'token already revoked' for already-revoked token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = DELETE('/user/tokens/missing00', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 404; assert resp1.json().error.message == 'token not found'; resp2 = DELETE('/user/tokens/revoked01', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 400; assert resp2.json().error.message == 'token already revoked'

test_cases.description: RevokePAT requires tokens:manage permission; PAT credential lacking it receives HTTP 403.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:read'] only
SQLite database has an active PAT with token_id 'abc12345' for this user
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; PAT is not revoked in the database
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=patWithOnlyTokensRead); assert resp.statusCode == 403; row = db.queryOne('SELECT revoked_at FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', 'abc12345'); assert row.revoked_at == null

test_cases.description: RevokePAT does not delete the PAT row from the pats table; it only sets revoked_at.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
User 'user-1' has an active PAT with token_id 'abc12345'
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:manage
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; the pats table still contains the row for abc12345 with revoked_at set to a non-null timestamp
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; row = db.queryOne('SELECT * FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', 'abc12345'); assert row != null; assert row.revoked_at != null

test_cases.description: ListPATs and GetPAT include expired PATs without filtering them out.
preconditions: SQLite database has an expired PAT for user-1 (expires_at is in the past)
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: Both endpoints return the expired PAT with its original expires_at value; HTTP 200 for both
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: listResp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=user1APIKey); assert any(p.token_id == expiredTokenID for p in listResp.json()); getResp = GET('/user/tokens/' + expiredTokenID, auth=user1APIKey); assert getResp.statusCode == 200; assert parseTime(getResp.json().expires_at) < now()

test_cases.description: ListPATs and GetPAT include revoked PATs without filtering them out.
preconditions: SQLite database has a revoked PAT for user-1 (revoked_at is set)
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: Both endpoints return the revoked PAT with non-null revoked_at; HTTP 200 for both
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: listResp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=user1APIKey); assert any(p.revoked_at != null and p.token_id == revokedTokenID for p in listResp.json()); getResp = GET('/user/tokens/' + revokedTokenID, auth=user1APIKey); assert getResp.statusCode == 200; assert getResp.json().revoked_at != null

test_cases.description: PAT lifecycle handlers (list, get, revoke) do not perform token expiry or revocation authentication checks; that is solely the auth middleware's responsibility.
preconditions: SQLite database has an expired PAT belonging to user-1
Auth middleware is stubbed to allow the request through as user-1
Authenticated as user-1 with tokens:read
test_cases.expected: GetPAT returns HTTP 200 with the expired PAT metadata; no handler-level expiry rejection occurs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens/' + expiredTokenID, auth=user1APIKey_stubMiddlewareAllowsThrough); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.json().token_id == expiredTokenID

property_tests.description: For any PAT created via POST /user/tokens, the pats table never stores the plaintext secret; only the SHA-256 hex hash is stored.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid CreatePATRequest inputs (valid name up to 255 chars, non-empty registered permissions subset, expires in {0,30,60,90})
property_tests.invariant_check: After POST /user/tokens succeeds (HTTP 201): extract secret from the token field (last segment after '_pat_<token_id>_'); compute expectedHash = sha256Hex(secret); query pats table for the row; assert row.secret_hash == expectedHash; assert the plaintext secret does not appear anywhere in the row fields

property_tests.description: For any PAT belonging to user A, GET and DELETE requests authenticated as user B (B != A) always return HTTP 404, never HTTP 200.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate pairs of distinct user IDs (userA, userB) and create PATs for userA; for each PAT token_id, send GET and DELETE requests authenticated as userB
property_tests.invariant_check: For all (userA_tokenID, userB_auth) pairs: GET('/user/tokens/' + userA_tokenID, auth=userB) returns statusCode 404 with message 'token not found'; DELETE('/user/tokens/' + userA_tokenID, auth=userB) returns statusCode 404 with message 'token not found'

property_tests.description: For any PAT creation request, the permissions array order is preserved in all subsequent responses (create, list, get, revoke).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary non-empty permutations of the registered permissions set (e.g., all orderings of ['users:read', 'orgs:read', 'tokens:read'])
property_tests.invariant_check: After POST /user/tokens with permissions in order P: createResp.permissions == P; listResp.find(t => t.token_id == id).permissions == P; getResp.permissions == P; revokeResp.permissions == P

property_tests.description: For any PAT credential with permissions set P, attempting to create a new PAT with permissions set Q: if Q is not a subset of P then HTTP 403 is returned; if Q is a subset of P then creation succeeds.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary permissions sets P (from the registered permissions set) as the creating PAT's permissions; generate arbitrary permissions sets Q; create PAT credentials with permissions P; attempt to create new PATs with permissions Q
property_tests.invariant_check: If Q is_subset_of P: response.statusCode == 201; if Q is_not_subset_of P: response.statusCode == 403 and response.json().error.message starts with 'cannot grant permission:'; if creating credential is API key: response.statusCode == 201 for any Q that is a subset of all registered permissions

property_tests.description: For any token_id, exactly one concurrent DELETE request receives HTTP 200; all others receive HTTP 400 'token already revoked'; revoked_at is set exactly once.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate an active PAT; dispatch N concurrent DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id requests (N in range 2..10)
property_tests.invariant_check: Count of 200 responses == 1; count of 400 responses == N-1; all 400 responses have message 'token already revoked'; db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats WHERE token_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NOT NULL', tokenID) == 1; loop terminates in bounded time (no deadlock)

property_tests.description: For any token returned in CreatePATResponse, the token field matches the regex ^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$ and the prefix segment equals apikit.TokenPrefix.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N successful POST /user/tokens calls (N in range 1..100) with arbitrary valid inputs
property_tests.invariant_check: For each response: matchesRegex(response.json().token, '^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$') == true; split(response.json().token, '_pat_')[0] == apikit.TokenPrefix; len(split(response.json().token, '_pat_')[1].split('_')[0]) == 8; len(split(response.json().token, '_')[last]) == 32

property_tests.description: For any HTTP response from any of the four PAT endpoints, the Cache-Control: no-store header is always present regardless of status code.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid and invalid requests to POST /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens, GET /user/tokens/:token_id, DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id (covering 200, 201, 400, 403, 404, 500 responses)
property_tests.invariant_check: For every response from any PAT endpoint: response.headers['Cache-Control'] == 'no-store'

property_tests.description: generateTokenID and generateSecret terminate within a bounded number of iterations (no unbounded retry loops on crypto/rand failure).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Instrument generateTokenID and generateSecret to count internal loop iterations; call each function N times (N in range 1..1000)
property_tests.invariant_check: Each call to generateTokenID completes in at most len(tokenID)=8 crypto/rand read operations; each call to generateSecret completes in at most len(secret)=32 crypto/rand read operations; no call blocks indefinitely; total iterations never exceed a fixed upper bound (e.g., 8*10 for tokenID, 32*10 for secret)

edge_case_tests.description: NewPATHandler panics with a descriptive message when either database or registry parameter is nil.
edge_case_tests.expected: Each nil parameter causes NewPATHandler to panic with a descriptive message identifying which parameter is nil
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: assertPanics(() => NewPATHandler(nil, validRegistry)); assertPanics(() => NewPATHandler(validDB, nil))

edge_case_tests.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 500 when crypto/rand fails during token generation; no token material is stored or returned.
preconditions: crypto/rand is mocked to return an error
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with message 'internal server error'; pats table is empty; no partial token is returned
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockRandToFail(); resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'; assert 'token' not in keys(resp.json()); rowCount = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats'); assert rowCount == 0

edge_case_tests.description: CreatePAT with expires=0 sets expires_at to null in the database and returns expires_at: null in the response.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 with expires_at: null in the response JSON; pats table row has expires_at = NULL
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'forever', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:0}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.json().expires_at == null; row = db.queryOne('SELECT expires_at FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', resp.json().token_id); assert row.expires_at == null

edge_case_tests.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 400 for negative expires values and values not in {0, 30, 60, 90}.
preconditions: Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry initialized
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with message 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90' for every listed value
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: for invalidExpires in [-1, 7, 29, 31, 59, 61, 89, 91, 365, 999]: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:invalidExpires}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'

edge_case_tests.description: CreatePAT returns the first validation error for permissions and stops at the first invalid permission (fail-fast, no error accumulation).
preconditions: Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read' but not 'widgets:delete' or 'gadgets:create'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with message for only the first offending permission ('unknown permission: widgets:delete'); second permission error not reported
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['widgets:delete','gadgets:create'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.json().error.message == 'unknown permission: widgets:delete'

edge_case_tests.description: A PAT with permissions [tokens:manage, users:read] can create a subset PAT but is rejected for permissions outside its own set.
preconditions: Authenticated credential is a PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage', 'users:read']
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:manage', 'users:read', 'keys:manage'
SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
edge_case_tests.expected: First request returns HTTP 201; second request returns HTTP 403 with message 'cannot grant permission: keys:manage'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'subset', permissions:['users:read'], expires:30}, auth=patWith_tokensManage_usersRead); assert resp1.statusCode == 201; resp2 = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'escalated', permissions:['users:read','keys:manage'], expires:30}, auth=patWith_tokensManage_usersRead); assert resp2.statusCode == 403; assert resp2.json().error.message == 'cannot grant permission: keys:manage'

edge_case_tests.description: CreatePAT returns HTTP 500 and rolls back when the db.WithTx transaction or INSERT fails; no partial state is persisted.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
db.WithTx is mocked to simulate a failed transaction (error returned from callback)
Authenticated user via API key
PermissionRegistry includes 'tokens:read'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with message 'internal server error'; pats table has no new rows; no token material in response
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: beforeCount = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats'); injectWithTxFailure(); resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'; afterCount = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats'); assert afterCount == beforeCount

edge_case_tests.description: Token generation (token_id and secret) occurs before the db.WithTx transaction opens so a failed transaction discards the generated values cleanly.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table
db.WithTx is mocked to fail after receiving the generated token values
Authenticated user via API key
edge_case_tests.expected: No token material (token_id, secret, hash) is persisted or returned in the error response; the generated token is discarded
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capturedTokenID = null; mockWithTxToCaptureThenFail(onCapture: (id) => capturedTokenID = id); resp = POST('/user/tokens', body={name:'test', permissions:['tokens:read'], expires:30}, auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; row = db.queryOne('SELECT * FROM pats WHERE token_id = ?', capturedTokenID); assert row == null

edge_case_tests.description: ListPATs returns HTTP 200 with an empty JSON array when the authenticated user has no PATs.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with the pats table (no rows for user-1)
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 200 with response body '[]' (empty JSON array), not null or HTTP 404
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.json() == []

edge_case_tests.description: ListPATs returns HTTP 500 with message 'internal server error' when the database query fails.
preconditions: Database query is mocked to return an error
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read

input.db_behavior: SELECT query on pats table returns an error

edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON body {"error":{"code":500,"message":"internal server error"}}
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockDBQueryToFail(); resp = GET('/user/tokens', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'

edge_case_tests.description: GetPAT returns HTTP 404 (not 403) when the token_id exists but belongs to a different user, to avoid information leakage.
preconditions: SQLite database has a PAT with token_id 'user2pat1' belonging to user-2
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:read
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404 with message 'token not found'; not HTTP 403
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/user/tokens/user2pat1', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.json().error.message == 'token not found'

edge_case_tests.description: GetPAT returns HTTP 500 with message 'internal server error' when the database query fails with a non-ErrNotFound error.
preconditions: Database query is mocked to return a generic error (not db.ErrNotFound)
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:read

input.db_behavior: SELECT query returns a generic database error

edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockDBQueryToReturnGenericError(); resp = GET('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'

edge_case_tests.description: Two concurrent revocation requests for the same token: the first receives HTTP 200, the second receives HTTP 400 'token already revoked'.
preconditions: SQLite database has an active PAT with token_id 'concurpat' for user-1
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:manage
edge_case_tests.expected: Exactly one request returns HTTP 200 with revoked_at set; the other returns HTTP 400 with 'token already revoked'; revoked_at is set exactly once in the database
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: results = concurrently([DELETE('/user/tokens/concurpat', auth=user1APIKey), DELETE('/user/tokens/concurpat', auth=user1APIKey)]); successCount = count(r for r in results if r.statusCode == 200); alreadyRevokedCount = count(r for r in results if r.statusCode == 400 and r.json().error.message == 'token already revoked'); assert successCount == 1; assert alreadyRevokedCount == 1; row = db.queryOne('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pats WHERE token_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NOT NULL', 'concurpat'); assert row.count == 1

edge_case_tests.description: RevokePAT returns HTTP 404 (not 403) when the token_id belongs to a different user, to avoid information leakage.
preconditions: SQLite database has an active PAT with token_id 'user2pat1' belonging to user-2
Authenticated as user-1 via API key with tokens:manage
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404 with message 'token not found'; not HTTP 403
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/user/tokens/user2pat1', auth=user1APIKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.json().error.message == 'token not found'

edge_case_tests.description: RevokePAT returns HTTP 500 with message 'internal server error' when the UPDATE or follow-up SELECT fails with a database error.
preconditions: Database UPDATE is mocked to return a generic error
Authenticated user via API key with tokens:manage

input.db_behavior: UPDATE statement returns a generic database error

edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockDBUpdateToReturnGenericError(); resp = DELETE('/user/tokens/abc12345', auth=apiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.json().error.message == 'internal server error'

edge_case_tests.description: PAT lifecycle handlers do not check blocked-user status; blocked-user enforcement is handled by auth middleware before handler code runs.
preconditions: User 'blocked-user' is marked as blocked in the users table
Auth middleware is configured to reject requests from blocked users with HTTP 403
blocked-user has a valid API key credential
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403 returned by auth middleware before ListPATs handler executes; no handler-level blocked-user check

smoke_tests.description: Happy path: create a new PAT using a valid API key credential and verify the full response including plaintext token.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /user/tokens with valid JSON body {name: 'ci-deploy', permissions: ['users:read', 'orgs:read'], expires: 90} and valid API key in Authorization header
expected_effects: HTTP 201 response is returned

Response body is CreatePATResponse with token_id (8 chars [a-z0-9]), name 'ci-deploy', token matching ^[a-z0-9]+_pat_[a-z0-9]{8}_[a-z0-9]{32}$, permissions ['users:read','orgs:read'], non-null expires_at, created_at
pats table contains exactly one new row with secret_hash = SHA-256(secret), permissions JSON ['users:read','orgs:read'], expires_at = created_at + 90*24h
Response header Cache-Control: no-store is present
No plaintext secret appears in the pats table row

smoke_tests.description: Happy path: list all PATs for the authenticated user returning metadata only.
smoke_tests.trigger: GET /user/tokens with valid API key in Authorization header; user has 2 existing PATs (one active, one revoked)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response is returned
Response body is a JSON array of 2 PATResponse objects
Array is ordered by created_at DESC (newest first)
No secret_hash, token, or expires_days fields appear in any array element
Revoked PAT has non-null revoked_at; active PAT has null revoked_at
Response header Cache-Control: no-store is present

smoke_tests.description: Happy path: get a single PAT by token_id and receive metadata only.
smoke_tests.trigger: GET /user/tokens/abc12345 with valid API key; user-1 owns PAT with token_id 'abc12345'
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response is returned
Response body is PATResponse with token_id 'abc12345', correct name, permissions (insertion order), created_at, expires_at, revoked_at
No secret_hash or token fields in response
Response header Cache-Control: no-store is present

smoke_tests.description: Happy path: revoke an active PAT and verify revoked_at is set in the response and database.
smoke_tests.trigger: DELETE /user/tokens/abc12345 with valid API key; user-1 owns active PAT with token_id 'abc12345'
real_components: SQLite conditional UPDATE on pats table
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response is returned
Response body is PATResponse with revoked_at set to a non-null timestamp
pats table row for token_id 'abc12345' has revoked_at set (row not deleted)
Response header Cache-Control: no-store is present

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end: create a PAT via API key, use the returned token as a PAT credential, then revoke it and verify audit trail.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /user/tokens with API key to create PAT with permissions ['tokens:manage','tokens:read']; then DELETE /user/tokens/:token_id using the returned PAT token; then GET /user/tokens to verify the revoked PAT appears in the listing
real_components: auth middleware (full PAT validation on revoke request)
expected_effects: CreatePAT returns HTTP 201 with plaintext token
RevokePAT using PAT credential returns HTTP 200 with revoked_at set
ListPATs returns array containing the revoked PAT with non-null revoked_at
pats table row persists with revoked_at set (not deleted)
All three responses have Cache-Control: no-store header
